twitter_thread: We blew $1.2M on AI software in Q1. $960k of it was for tools used less than 5 times.
UBER's $100M budget blowout is a warning. Our audit found 30% of AI costs came from licenses idle for 6+ months.
AWS SageMaker costs $2.50/hr, Google Cloud AI Platform costs $3.06/hr. These add up fast.
Zero-based budgeting helped us cut AI software spend by 25%. It can do the same for you.
Don't assume 10 employees need 10 licenses. We found 3 employees can share 1 license for certain tools.
Implement a 90-day license review to avoid wasting $10k/month on unused AI software.
What's the most surprising AI software cost you've encountered in your organization? #ai #budgeting
pack.linkedin: Our company's $1.2M Q1 AI software spend was 80% wasteful. A thorough audit revealed $960k was spent on barely used tools. AWS SageMaker and Google Cloud AI Platform can quickly add up to thousands of dollars if not monitored. By implementing zero-based budgeting and regular license reviews, we cut AI software spend by 25%. What specific steps has your organization taken to prevent similar waste?
tiktok_hooks: We wasted $960k on AI software that was used less than 5 times
You're probably overspending on AI software by thousands of dollars per month
Our AI budget was 80% fat, don't let yours be too
pack.reddit_headline: UBER's $100M budget blowout: a symptom of the AI industry's cost opacity problem?
